你都怎麼學語言？ 除了每天跟我語言學校的朋友聊天交流日文之外 我自己平常就是用 Galaxy Tab S11 Ultra 的這幾招學習 1.只要遇到不會的單字就派出搜尋圈，隨時翻譯學超快的！ 2.用 Samsung Notes 搭配 S Pen，練習手寫還有各種講義🥰 3.用 Gemini AI 助理幫我出考題！直接省去講義考題費！Gemini Live 的情境模擬也超實用💕 這台 Samsung Galaxy Tab S11 Ultra 就推推給你們囉🥰 目標是通過N3~希望年底可以先考過N5！ #GalaxyTabS11Ultra #GalaxyAI @samsungtaiwan

yun_the_Pineapple_

You sent an attachment.
Hey everyone, Johnny here to explain. This meme humorously uses a progression of images featuring a giant inflatable rubber duck in various states of inflation and distress to create a visual scale for expressing how someone is feeling, ranging from 1 (fully inflated, upright, and cheerful) to 9 (completely deflated and collapsed in a puddle), essentially acting as an emotional barometer. Each numbered photo represents a different mood or level of functioning, with the duck at "1" looking perfectly happy and buoyant, suggesting a great or very good day, while as the numbers increase, the duck becomes progressively more deflated, saggy, or awkwardly shaped, representing increasing levels of exhaustion, struggle, or emotional collapse. By the time we get to 9, the duck is just a sad, flattened heap on the water, clearly done with everything, symbolizing complete burnout or despair. The humor lies in the absurdity and relatability—everyone has had days where they feel like a "5" (stretched thin), a "7" (barely holding it together), or a full-blown "9" (emotionally pancaked). The meme invites viewers to self-assess their current state in a lighthearted, non-verbal way using this oddly expressive rubber duck scale. Since you've read thus far, you might as well follow @johnny.explains since I explain memes for you all the time. #explorepage #dankmemes #cancerousmemes #funnymemes #lmao #funny #dailymemes #viral #fypage #fyp #hilarious #humor #spicymemes
johnny.explains

#🔨ᴿᵉᵃᵈʸ ᵗᵒ ᵇᵘᶦˡᵈ ᵗʰᵉ ᶠᵒᵘⁿᵈᵃᵗᶦᵒⁿ ᵒᶠ ʸᵒᵘʳ ᵃᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ ᶜʳᵃᶠᵗ? ᴶᵒᶦⁿ ᵘˢ ᶦⁿ-ᵖᵉʳˢᵒⁿ ᵃᵗ ᵒᵘʳ ᴸᵒˢ ᴬⁿᵍᵉˡᵉˢ ˢᵗᵘᵈᶦᵒ ᶠᵒʳ ᵗʰᵉ ⁶-ᵂᵉᵉᵏ ᴬᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ ᵀᵉᶜʰⁿᶦᵠᵘᵉ ᵖʳᵒᵍʳᵃᵐ, ᵗʰᵉ ᵉˢˢᵉⁿᵗᶦᵃˡ ᶠᶦʳˢᵗ ˢᵗᵉᵖ ᶠᵒʳ ᵃˡˡ ˢᵗᵘᵈᵉⁿᵗˢ ᵃᵗ ᵗʰᵉ ˢᵗᵘᵈᶦᵒ.🌟 ᴵⁿ ᵗʰᶦˢ ᶜᵒᵐᵖʳᵉʰᵉⁿˢᶦᵛᵉ ᶜᵒᵘʳˢᵉ, ʸᵒᵘ’ˡˡ ᵉˣᵖˡᵒʳᵉ: 🔹• ᴴᵒʷᵃʳᵈ ᶠᶦⁿᵉ’ˢ ˢᶜʳᶦᵖᵗ ᴬⁿᵃˡʸˢᶦˢ ᴸᵉᶜᵗᵘʳᵉ 🔹• ᵁᵗᵃ ᴴᵃᵍᵉⁿ’ˢ ᴼᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ ᴱˣᵉʳᶜᶦˢᵉˢ 🔹• ᴾᵉʳˢᵒⁿᵃˡᶦᶻᵃᵗᶦᵒⁿ, ˢᵉⁿˢᵒʳʸ ʷᵒʳᵏ, ᵃⁿᵈ ᵉᵐᵒᵗᶦᵒⁿᵃˡ ʳᵉᶜᵃˡˡ ʷᶦᵗʰ ᴰᵃᵛᶦᵈ ᶜᵒᵘʳʸ 🔹• ᴴᵒʷ ᵗᵒ ʰᵃⁿᵈˡᵉ ⁿᵉʳᵛᵉˢ ᵃⁿᵈ ᵇʳᵉᵃᵏ ᵒˡᵈ ʰᵃᵇᶦᵗˢ 🔹• ᴬˢˢᶦᵍⁿᵉᵈ ᵐᵃᵗᵉʳᶦᵃˡ ᵃⁿᵈ ᵃⁿ ᶦⁿᵗʳᵒ ᵗᵒ ˢᶜᵉⁿᵉ ˢᵗᵘᵈʸ ᵀʰᶦˢ ᶜᵒᵘʳˢᵉ ᵍᵒᵉˢ ᵇᵉʸᵒⁿᵈ ᵃᵘᵈᶦᵗᶦᵒⁿ ᵖʳᵉᵖ, ᶠᵒᶜᵘˢᶦⁿᵍ ᵒⁿ ᵗʰᵉ ᵉⁿᵗᶦʳᵉ ᶜʳᵃᶠᵗ ᵒᶠ ᵃᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ ᵗᵒ ʰᵉˡᵖ ʸᵒᵘ ⁿᵒᵗ ᵒⁿˡʸ ᵇᵒᵒᵏ ᵗʰᵉ ʲᵒᵇ ᵇᵘᵗ ⁿᵃᶦˡ ᵗʰᵉ ʳᵒˡᵉ ᵒⁿ ˢᵉᵗ ᵒʳ ˢᵗᵃᵍᵉ.🎥 📚ᴿᵉᵠᵘᶦʳᵉᵈ ᵀᵉˣᵗˢ: 🔹• ᴬ ᶜʰᵃˡˡᵉⁿᵍᵉ ᶠᵒʳ ᵗʰᵉ ᴬᶜᵗᵒʳ ᵇʸ ᵁᵗᵃ ᴴᵃᵍᵉⁿ 🔹• ᶠᶦⁿᵉ ᴼⁿ ᴬᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ: ᴬ ⱽᶦˢᶦᵒⁿ ᵒᶠ ᵗʰᵉ ᶜʳᵃᶠᵗ ᵇʸ ᴴᵒʷᵃʳᵈ ᶠᶦⁿᵉ ᴮᵉ ˢᵘʳᵉ ᵗᵒ ᵍʳᵃᵇ ʸᵒᵘʳ ᵇᵒᵒᵏˢ ᵇᵉᶠᵒʳᵉ ᵗʰᵉ ᶠᶦʳˢᵗ ᶜˡᵃˢˢ ᵃⁿᵈ ˢᵗᵃʳᵗ ʳᵉᵃᵈᶦⁿᵍ ᴴᵒʷᵃʳᵈ’ˢ ᵇᵒᵒᵏ ᵗᵒ ᵈᶦᵛᵉ ᶦⁿᵗᵒ ᵗʰᶦˢ ᵗʳᵃⁿˢᶠᵒʳᵐᵃᵗᶦᵒⁿᵃˡ ᵉˣᵖᵉʳᶦᵉⁿᶜᵉ 📖 ➡️ᶜˡᵃˢˢ ᵇᵉᵍᶦⁿˢ ᴼᶜᵗᵒᵇᵉʳ ¹⁵ᵗʰ ᵃᵗ ¹⁰ ᵃᵐ ᴾᵀ ˢᵉᶜᵘʳᵉ ʸᵒᵘʳ ˢᵖᵒᵗ ᵗᵒᵈᵃʸ 🥰✨ #techwaseem #explorepage #reelsviral #fyp

𝐈𝐧 𝐭𝐨𝐝𝐚𝐲'𝐬 𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐨𝐟 #𝟏𝟎𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬, 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐝 𝐚 𝐜𝐨𝐳𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐚𝐭 𝐡𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬 @𝐲𝐚𝐳𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝 (𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐲) 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐬𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐩𝐭𝐬, 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐤 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐝𝐨𝐠 𝐊𝐨𝐤𝐨 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐚𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚 𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐦 𝐜𝐮𝐩 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐞𝐚 (𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐟𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐬𝐜𝐨𝐨𝐩𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐬𝐮𝐠𝐚𝐫, 𝐧𝐨 𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬)🐾💗 "𝐌𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐯𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐝 𝐛𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐈 𝐠𝐞𝐭 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐰𝐚𝐥𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐬𝐞𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐞'𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐝𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲," 𝐬𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧. "𝐈 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐟𝐞𝐞𝐥 𝐥𝐢𝐤𝐞 𝐈 𝐟𝐢𝐭 𝐢𝐧 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐥𝐲." 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧, 𝐰𝐡𝐨 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐢𝐧 "𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐫" 𝐨𝐧 @𝐧𝐞𝐭𝐟𝐥𝐢𝐱𝐮𝐤 𝐚𝐥𝐬𝐨 𝐫𝐞𝐟𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐨𝐧 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐞𝐱𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐢𝐥𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝟑, 𝐰𝐡𝐢𝐜𝐡 𝐫𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐰𝐞𝐞𝐤. "𝐁𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐚𝐬 𝐬𝐨 𝐠𝐨𝐨𝐝 𝐛𝐞𝐜𝐚𝐮𝐬𝐞 𝐈'𝐦 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐦𝐲𝐬𝐞𝐥𝐟, 𝐛𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲. 𝐈'𝐦 𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐝 𝐨𝐟 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲 𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧.

If you love ads like this, Follow @iconiq.ads ❤️ so you don’t miss the next one 😉 This is hands-down one of the funniest commercials to come out recently. They got the actual Logan Roy, the Succession star, to enroll as a “college freshman,” and his whole deal is that he’s only there for the student discounts on Uber and Uber Eats. He’s completely miserable trying to fit in, terrorizing his young roommate, and interrupting class just to noisily slurp a smoothie—all while preaching about how good the savings are. The best part is his signature, curmudgeonly attitude contrasting with campus life. When someone asks what he’s majoring in, he just glares and says his interests are “food and saving money on food.” It’s pure comedic gold. It really does drive home their point, though: the savings on $0 Delivery Fees and up to 10% off on Uber Eats, plus the ride credits, must be that good if a fictional billionaire would subject himself to dorm life for it. #UberOne #UberOneForStudents #BrianCox #Succession #CollegeLife #StudentDiscounts
iconiq.ads

Wan 2.2 Animate can turn a single photo into a lifelike video — with precision accurate enough to mimic deepfakes. 🔥 It tracks facial geometry, eye movement, and lip sync in real time, creating avatars that look seamlessly human — without heavy GPU power. This breakthrough signals the rapid rise of consumer-level visual synthesis — a glimpse into the future of AI-powered content creation. 👨👩 Parents and kids often clash because they see the world through different lenses. Parents rely on their experiences and want to protect their children, while kids crave independence and want to explore on their own terms. These opposing needs can make even simple decisions feel like power struggles. Communication gaps also play a big role. Parents may feel disrespected when kids don’t listen, while kids may feel misunderstood or controlled. Without patience and empathy on both sides, these misunderstandings can build up, leading to arguments and emotional distance. Like this content? Follow us for more @memezar ❤️ #memezar #relatable

🎬 Fun Fact: SpongeBob SquarePants (1999– ) The Krusty Krab, where SpongeBob works, isn’t actually a crab trap as many assume — the building’s design was based on a New England–style lobster trap. Creator Stephen Hillenburg added this detail as a subtle nod to his marine biology background. Fans often misinterpret it as a crab cage, but in reality, it reflects a traditional ocean tool, tying the restaurant’s look directly to real maritime culture.

⠀ sent an attachment.
Japan converts footsteps into electricity using piezoelectric tiles. These tiles capture the kinetic energy from your steps. When you walk, your weight and motion create pressure on the tiles. The tiles bend slightly, producing mechanical stress. Piezoelectric materials inside the tiles convert this stress into electrical energy. Each step generates a small charge, and millions of steps together produce enough power to run LED lights, digital displays, and sensors. In busy places like Shibuya Station, around 2.4 million footsteps a day contribute to this system. The electricity is stored or used immediately, reducing reliance on traditional power sources and supporting sustainable urban infrastructure. This method turns everyday motion into practical renewable energy. #japan #knowledge #facts #didyouknow #fyp #technology #innovation #history #tech #experiments #viral

“When you type ‘sudo rm -rf /’ instead of ‘sudo rm -rf ./’”. 📺 VA 144Hz vs OLED 360Hz: Which is Right for You? 🎮 🔍 VA 144Hz 💡 Pros: ✅ High contrast, deep blacks – perfect for cinematic games. ✅ Budget-friendly, great value for money. ❌ Cons: ⚠️ Slower response times, may show smearing in fast scenes. ⚠️ Limited viewing angles. 🌟 OLED 360Hz 💡 Pros: 🚀 Near-instant response (0.03ms) – crystal-clear motion! 🎨 Perfect blacks, vibrant visuals, wide viewing angles. 🎮 Ideal for competitive gaming (Valorant, CS:GO). ❌ Cons: ⚠️ Risk of burn-in with static content. ⚠️ Higher price tag. ⚡ 144Hz or 360Hz? 🎯 VA 144Hz – Great for casual or story-driven games, especially on a budget. 🏆 OLED 360Hz – The ultimate choice for competitive gamers with a high-end PC! 💬 Which one’s your pick? Share in the comments! 👇 #gaming #monitor #tech #oled #vamonitor #screens #display #instagram #instadaily #reels #usa #usa🇺🇸 📌 Pro Tip: For the perfect balance, a 1440p 240Hz OLED could be the sweet spot! 🎮✨

You sent an attachment.
Chanakya - The Emotion of Courage and Valor (Veer Rasa)A moment from the sold-out Sitar for Mental Health live show in Mumbai (NCPA), performing my composition “Chanakya.”Inspired by the NavRasa, this piece expresses ‘Veer Rasa’-the essence of courage and strength. While a hero may not need weapons, for me, the sitar is my instrument of choice to inspire peace and consciousness.This song is deeply special to me because it channels the warrior spirit within, resonating with the same guidance and strength that the great kingmaker in history Chanakya bestowed upon his disciple Chandragupta Maurya and made him king of the Indian Subcontinent.True gallantry is born from within. Stay strong keep ya head up🫶🏻#SitarForMental Health #VeerRasa #NavRasa #Indian ClassicalMusic #Live Performance #sitar #Healing Through Music #Chanakya #meditation

You sent an attachment.
The Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI) is a personality tool that helps you understand yourself and others better. It sorts people into 16 unique personality types based on how we energize, perceive, decide, and live our lives. Each type is a combination of four preferences: Introversion or Extraversion, Sensing or Intuition, Thinking or Feeling, and Judging or Perceiving. Knowing your MBTI type is like getting a glimpse into your inner world — how you process emotions, handle challenges, and connect with people. It’s not about putting you in a box but giving you language to describe your strengths, quirks, and ways of seeing the world. Whether you’re an analytical INTJ, a creative INFP, or an outgoing ESFP, MBTI celebrates the beautiful diversity in how we all experience life. It’s a reminder that everyone’s mind works differently and that’s what makes the world interesting. So, here’s to embracing who we are—flaws, passions, and all—and learning to understand others with empathy. Drop your type below, and let’s celebrate our unique vibes! #MBTIVibes #PersonalityTypeThings #MyMBTIType #MBTILife #MBTIAesthetic #MBTICore #MBTICommunity #MBTILovers #MBTIMood #InsideMyType

You sent an attachment.
Hey everyone, Johnny here to explain. This meme humorously plays on the hype surrounding the PlayStation 5 (PS5), which is known for its sleek white-and-blue packaging and has been a highly sought-after item since its release. The image shows someone carrying a large box that, at first glance, resembles a PS5 box due to its shape and color scheme. The caption, "I finally got it homies... My Platestation 5," sets up the expectation that the person has finally acquired the coveted gaming console. However, the punchline is revealed in the second image where it turns out to be a box for a dish rack labeled "Dish Rack" — a household item used for drying plates. The humor lies in the pun “Platestation 5,” a play on "PlayStation 5," suggesting a console for plates instead of games. It’s a clever mix of visual misdirection and wordplay, poking fun at the excitement around tech gadgets while contrasting it with something as mundane as kitchenware. Since you've read thus far, you might as well follow @johnny.explains since I explain memes for you all the time. #funnymemes #explorepage #dankmemes #cancerousmemes #lmao #memes #funny #viral #dailymemes #fypage #fyp #hilarious #humor #spicymemes
johnny.explains
